normalization_point: Loop 10 lowers battery-material scores because EV CAPA, idle plants, and contract cancellations can quickly become 4C.
Battery equipment is Watch-to-Green only when CAPEX becomes delivery, revenue, and margin.
Recycling/second-life stays Watch-to-Green but now requires SOH and grading-cost validation.
Second-life battery storage is not simple recycling: SOH, residual capacity, safety, warranty, and customer contracts decide visibility.
ESS LFP grid storage is split out because value, duration, customer, and GWh can make a cleaner Stage 2/3 path.
Tesla Megapack confirmation can upgrade an undisclosed LFP contract to Stage 2, but Green still needs ramp-up, ESS OPM, utilization, and FCF.
AI data-center storage has strong demand but stays Watch until customer contracts, GWh, margin, and FCF are visible.
EV-to-ESS redeployment is useful only if the idle EV fixed-cost problem is offset by real ESS contracts and margins.
SK On-Ford-style JV restructuring is a Watch path: fixed-cost relief and ESS pivot can help, but EV-demand 4C remains attached.
EV CAPA contract cancellation is a 4C/RedTeam gate; it must not be averaged into positive battery growth evidence.
Large battery contracts get a Stage 3 cap when customer, use-case, value, or margin is not disclosed.
EV infrastructure needs utilization and unit economics, not charger-count headlines.
Hydrogen CAPEX can reach Stage 2, but Green needs customers, utilization, and OP conversion.
Solar stays RedTeam-first because subsidy and US manufacturing can break on customs or supply-chain detention.
Wind/PPA policy stories remain Watch until project economics survive rates, permits, and foundation costs.
Waste treatment remains the clearest R3 Green-capable axis when permits, volume, and recurring FCF are proven.
Carbon price is not enough; compliance revenue and cost pass-through are the stronger route.
Data-center water reuse needs named customers and economics before Green.
EV/BESS fire, certification, insurance, and facility-permitting overlay is a RedTeam gate, not positive score.
Moss Landing-style BESS fire shows storage demand can still fail on safety, permitting, insurance, and local acceptance.
SOH opacity is a battery recycling and second-life RedTeam gate, not a Green input.
Graphite security is a supply-chain Watch axis, not automatic Green: cost, financing, and offtake must be proven.
Lithium ESS demand is a cycle overlay by default; ESS demand can help Stage 1/2 but does not make Green alone.
Sodium-ion is a RedTeam overlay because it can cap lithium/LFP economics in low-cost ESS or low-cost EV use cases.
Solid-state licensing can be Stage 2, but Green waits for production, vehicle adoption, royalty revenue, cost, and yield.
Speculative solid-state or new battery technology cannot be Green before commercialization, customer evidence, and FCF.
